summary: Permission-based UI Rendering testing completed. Backend: 15/15 tests PASSED (100%). Frontend: All E2E tests passed - Owner sees all 6 tabs, regular member correctly sees only 2 tabs (Members, Vault) with restricted actions.

issues: React hydration warning: <span> cannot be child of <select>/<option> - cosmetic only, does not affect functionality (carried over from previous iteration)

critical_code_review_comments: Good: GET /api/organizations/{org_id}/my-permissions returns correct permissions structure with base_role, custom_role, source fields
Good: Owner gets all 23 permissions with source='owner'
Good: Default member gets 4 permissions (documents:view, vault:view, members:view, templates:view) with source='default_member'
Good: Custom role assignment correctly changes effective permissions and source
Good: Tab filtering works correctly - TABS = ALL_TABS.filter(tab => isAdmin || myPerms.includes(tab.permission))
Good: PermissionGate component properly hides/shows content based on permissions
Good: PermissionGate showLock=true correctly shows 'Restricted' lock indicator with Lock icon
Good: Permission source badge displays correctly showing 'owner', 'default_member', or custom role name
Good: Role management dropdowns only visible when user has members:manage_roles permission
Good: Vault upload button gated by vault:upload permission
Good: usePermissions hook properly caches permissions per org to avoid refetching

success_rate.frontend: 100% - all permission UI elements working correctly

seed_data_creation: Invited demo@test.com to TEST_RBAC_SSO Organization as member for testing restricted view
context_for_next_testing_agent: Permission-based UI rendering fully working. Owner sees all 6 tabs (Members, Roles, Vault, Invites, SSO, Settings). Regular member (default_member role) sees only 2 tabs (Members, Vault). Invite button shows 'Restricted' lock for members without members:invite permission. Role dropdowns only visible to users with members:manage_roles. Settings tab shows permissions summary with count and source (e.g., '23 permissions via owner'). The PermissionGate component supports: permission (single), permissions (any of), allOf (all of) modes.
